answer:
1. Click the Pull requests tab of your hello-world repository.
2. Click New pull request.
3. In the Example Comparisons box, select the branch you made, readme-edits, to compare with main (the original).
4. Look over your changes in the diffs on the Compare page, make sure they're what you want to submit.
5. Click Create pull request.
6. Give your pull request a title and write a brief description of your changes.
7. Click Create pull request.
8. At the bottom of the pull request, click Merge pull request to merge the changes into main.
9. Click Confirm merge.
10. Click Delete branch.

GitHub will prevent merging a pull request if there are conflicts between the code in the pull request and the existing code on the main branch. The two ways to resolve this issue are to make a commit that resolves the conflicts or use comments in the pull request to discuss the conflicts with team members.

The default branch name is "main" and the branch name created in the tutorial is "readme-edits". The "readme-edits" branch was created to make changes to the README file without affecting the main branch, allowing for experimentation and editing before committing the changes to the main branch.
A license file and a README file are two of the options, but the document does not specify the third. The tutorial instructs the user to include a README file.
